zh.St Peter's Church, Frocester, Gloucester: 英國格盧斯特斯爾福洛斯特聖 比特也斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Shaw Lehman於1467年出生在英國賀福爾賀福德時，她的父親Sir Edmund Shaw III33歲，母親Juliana Munday Browne24歲。她於1500年之前與Edward de Lugg在英國英格蘭賀福爾盧沃迪結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。她去世於1552年1月10日在她的家鄉享年85歲。

zh-hans.St Peter's Church, Frocester, Gloucester: 英国格盧斯特斯爾福洛斯特聖 比特也斯 邱奇
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Shaw Lehman于1467年出生在英国賀福爾賀福德时，她的父亲Sir Edmund Shaw III33岁，母亲Juliana Munday Browne24岁。她于1500年之前与Edward de Lugg在英国英格兰賀福爾盧沃迪结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。她去世于1552年1月10日在她的家乡享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: Shaw
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English s(c)hawe, s(c)haghe ‘small wood, grove, thicket’ (Old English sceaga). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a small wood, or habitational, for someone from any of the many places so named. Shaw and Shawe are most frequent in Lancashire and Yorkshire, where Shaw in Oldham (Lancashire) may be a principal source of the surname. The English and Lowland Scottish surname was also established in Ireland in the 17th century.
Scottish: shortened form of various surnames from the Gaelic personal name Sitheach, derived from sithech ‘wolf’.
Irish (Down and Antrim): adopted for Ó Síthigh ‘descendant of Sítheach’, a personal name based on sítheach ‘peaceful’. Compare Sheehy .

variants.name: Show
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cumberland): probably a variant of Shaw , perhaps representing a blend in Middle English of Old English sceaga and Old Norse skógr, both meaning ‘wood’.
Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Schau  .

variants.name: Sheehy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, Aisling, Brigid, Declan, Egan, Eoin, Finbar, Ronan.

Irish (Limerick and Kerry): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Síthigh, ‘descendant of Sítheach’, or Mac Síthigh ‘son of Sítheach’, a form of the Irish personal name Síodhach ‘peaceful’, from the adjective sítheach ‘peaceful’ or ‘other worldly’ (see Sheehan ). The noun síth, from which this adjective derives, means basically ‘settlement’, hence both ‘peace’ and ‘place inhabited by other world beings’. This name was established in Ireland from the early 14th century, when a branch of the Scottish McDonnells, descended from Sítheach, great-grandson of Domhnaill, settled in the country.

nameMeanings.name: Lehman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Lehmann  . Compare   Layman  ,   Leaman  ,   Leeman  , and   Leman  .

History: This (see 1 above) is the name of a Swiss Mennonite family, originating in the Emmental in the canton of Bern, Switzerland, from where many of its bearers moved in the early 18th century to the Palatinate in Germany, Alsace in France, and (finally) to PA in the US. Its original Swiss German form is Lehmann. Among the American Mennonites it is also established in the altered forms Layman and Leaman.

variants.name: Lehmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Fritz, Klaus, Gerhard, Arno, Gunter, Juergen, Almut.

German: status name for a feudal tenant or vassal, Middle High German lēheman, lēnman (from lēhen ‘to hold land as a feudal tenant’ + man ‘man’). The tenant held land on loan for the duration of his life in return for rent or service, but was not free to transfer or divide it. See also Lehman .
Jewish (western Ashkenazic): from the personal name Lehmann, perhaps related to Leo 3.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Shaw Lehman was born in 1467, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, her father, Sir Edmund Shaw III, was 33 and her mother, Juliana Munday Browne, was 24. She married Edward de Lugg before 1500, in Lugwardine, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. She died on 10 January 1552, in her hometown, at the age of 85.

ko.St Peter's Church, Frocester, Gloucester: 영국 그루스스터실르 프록스터 스트 피터'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Shaw Lehman(이)가 1467년 영국 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Edmund Shaw III(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Juliana Munday Browne(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1500년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 루그워딘에서 Edward de Lugg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1552년 1월 10일 그녀의 고향에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward de Lugg перед 1500 г. в местности лугвардаин, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Она умерла 10 января 1552 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 85 лет.
